title: cistus marifolius var. vinealis
intro: Cistus marifolius var. vinealis, a subspecies of the rockrose plant, is a flowering shrub native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its small white or pink flowers and fragrant leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Cistus marifolium var. vinealis include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Cistus marifolium var. vinealis is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and optimal usage.
